{{Quote|Fenlish, Khana, each of you choose four and follow.|Cadet commander Shawnkyr Nuruodo, to Fenlish and Khana|<ref name="RSBF" />}}
'''Fenlish''' was a [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Cadet/Legends|cadet]] who was a member of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy's]]<ref name="RSBF" /> [[Chaf family/Legends|Chaf family]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> and studied<ref name="RSBF" /> at the [[Syndic Mitth'raw'nuruodo training academy]],<ref name="NJO">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Journey]]''</ref> which was located on the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="RSBF" /> [[Rhigar/Legends|Rhigar]].<ref name="TEA" /> The cadet served the [[Empire of the Hand]].<ref name="RSBF" />

In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],<ref name="Date">The events of ''[[Red Sky, Blue Flame]]'' take place in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''.</ref> when the academy was [[Battle of Rhigar|attacked]] by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[cadet commander]] [[Shawnkyr Nuruodo]] [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] Fenlish and the cadet [[Khana]] to gather eight [[Charric/Legends|charric]] [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] and [[Power cell/Legends|energy packs]]. The cadets ran to a nearby weapons cache, which they could not open, asking Nuruodo to open it; however, she was unable to do so. [[Human/Legends|Human]] cadet [[Jagged Fel]] then opened the cache with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], giving its contents to the cadet commander. Shortly after, under Nuruodo's directive, Fenlish and the other cadets fled to the academy's [[Forest/Legends|forests]]. The battle ended with the pirates' defeat.<ref name="RSBF" />

==Behind the scenes==
Fenlish appeared in the short story "[[Red Sky, Blue Flame]]," which was written by [[Elaine Cunningham]] and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 7|seventh issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="RSBF" /> on [[October 16]], [[2001]].<ref name="Rd">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20011018.html|text=Live on the Fringe: ''Gamer'' # 7}}</ref>
